context: Frequency:   6-11%; in 4.3% bilateral (more frequent in recurrent disease) ◊  Most commonly in nodular sclerosing type ◊  Subsequent to hilar adenopathy in ipsilateral lung √  coarse reticulonodular pattern contiguous with mediastinum = direct extension from mediastinal nodes along lymphatics √  nodular parenchymal lesions √  miliary nodules √  endobronchial involvement √  lobar atelectasis ← endobronchial obstruction (rare) √   cavitation ← necrosis (rare) √  circumscribed subpleural masses √  pleural effusion (20-50%) ← lymphatic obstruction √  diffuse nonsegmental infiltrate (pneumonic type
